name: Artemis I
description: Uncrewed test flight. Orion traveled 268,000 miles from Earth, 40,000 miles beyond the Moon. 25.5 days in flight. Splashdown Dec 11 2022.

name: Artemis II
description: First crewed Artemis flight. 9-day cislunar free-return trajectory. Lunar flyby April 6. Distance record: 406,771 km (252,756 mi) — farthest humans have ever traveled. First crewed flight beyond LEO since Apollo 17.

name: Artemis III
description: Redesigned mission: crewed Earth-orbit HLS docking test. Crew will evaluate SpaceX Starship HLS and Blue Origin Blue Moon, test AxEMU spacesuits in LEO. Not a lunar landing.

name: Artemis IV
description: First crewed lunar landing. First woman and first person of color on the Moon. SpaceX Starship HLS. South Pole landing region. First Gateway docking.
vehicle: SLS Block 1B + Orion + SpaceX Starship HLS

name: Artemis V
description: Blue Origin National Team HLS debut. Second crewed lunar landing. Gateway fully docked.
vehicle: SLS Block 1B + Orion + Blue Origin HLS

name: Artemis VI
description: Extended lunar surface operations. Gateway fully operational. Multi-week surface stay.

name: Artemis VII
description: Sustained presence. Multi-week surface operations. Permanent foothold at the lunar south pole.
